raw_consumer_statement:
I initially contacted Citi Bank to cancel my AAdvantage Platinum credit card since I was no longer using it and they were charging a {$99.00} annual fee. The representative informed me that rather than cancelling the card and impacting my credit, I could just downgrade to the AAdvantage XXXX credit card so that I could retain the credit line while avoiding any annual fees. A month and a half later I receive a {$99.00} annual charge associated with the Platinum card on my XXXX card statement, so I contacted Citi Bank customer service to have this resolved and was informed that the fee would only be refunded after 51 days, and to avoid any late payments affecting my credit history, I would need to pay the {$99.00} first, then they would refund it in 51 days, and after a few months of no usage they would eventually cut me a refund check. 
XXXX ; If I would've just cancelled my credit card in XXXX I could have avoided this mess, now I will need to pay {$99.00} annual fee for which I am receiving no benefits, and wait approximately 3-6 months before Citi Bank decides to cut a refund check, all to avoid a negative mark on my completely clean credit history.
